hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is first pretrained on a data-rich domain of additive-free SEM images and then fine-tuned on a data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then the resulting two-stage transfer learning model will achieve higher classification accuracy for NCM cathode composition and aging state identification compared to models trained without pretraining or with fine-tuning alone, because the source domain pretraining enables the network to learn generalizable morphological feature representations that compensate for the scarcity of labeled target domain data.
